chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, Bench at Sukkur C. P. No. D – 1075 of 2018 (Wajid Hussain V/S Province of Sindh & others) Before: Mr. Justice Muhammad Junaid Ghaffar Mr. Justice Zulfiqar Ali Sangi Date of Hearing: 03-03-2022 Date of Decision: 03-03-2022 Mr. Muhammad Raza Soomro, Advocate for the Petitioner. Mr. Zulfiqar Ali Naich, Assistant Advocate General-Sindh. O R D E R Muhammad Junaid Ghaffar, J. – Through this Constitutional Petition, the Petitioner has prayed for the following relief(s): “a). Direct the respondents to issue appointment order to the petitioner for the post of Junior Clerk BPS 07 as he is found fit for such post like others”.. 2. Case of the Petitioner is that pursuant to some advertisement, an offer letter was issued to him on 26.12.2012 and immediately thereafter some ban was imposed on the recruitment process, hence the Petitioner was not appointed. It has been argued that even after lifting of the ban, the Petitioner was not appointed. 3. Petitioner’s Counsel in support of his case has relied upon certain orders passed by different Benches of this Court in similar circumstances and has prayed for grant of relief. 4. Insofar as Petitioner’s case is concerned, the appointment process in question was initiated in 2012; whereas, this Petition has been filed in 2018 and as to involvement of laches, Counsel for the Petitioner has not been able to satisfactorily respond. Insofar as orders passed in other CP No.D-1075 of 2018 2 cases are concerned, they are not directly applicable on the facts and circumstances of the present case, hence distinguishable; whereas, appointment process has now been completed and as per comments, no vacancies are available in respect of the appointment process in question. 5.

Insofar as Petitioner’s case is concerned, the appointment process in question was initiated in 2012; whereas, this Petition has been filed in 2018 and as to involvement of laches, Counsel for the Petitioner has not been able to satisfactorily respond. Insofar as orders passed in other CP No.D-1075 of 2018 2 cases are concerned, they are not directly applicable on the facts and circumstances of the present case, hence distinguishable; whereas, appointment process has now been completed and as per comments, no vacancies are available in respect of the appointment process in question. 5. In view of such position, instant Petition is hit by laches; therefore, the same is not maintainable and is hereby dismissed. J U D G E J U D G E Ahmad
